package.brief: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The meeting reviewed bilateral relations and discussed ways to expand cooperation, including economic ties. Regional developments in the Middle East were also on the agenda. HH the Amir emphasized Qatar's commitment to strengthening ties with Pakistan.

article.headline: HH the Amir Receives Prime Minister of Pakistan at Amiri Diwan
article.body:
**Doha, Qatar – 7 June 2026** – HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday.

During the meeting, the two leaders reviewed bilateral relations between Qatar and Pakistan and discussed ways to enhance cooperation across various fields, according to a statement from the Amiri Diwan.

HH the Amir expressed Qatar's appreciation for Pakistan's stance on regional and international issues, and underscored the importance of deepening economic cooperation between the two countries.

"We value Pakistan's stance and look forward to deeper economic cooperation between our two countries," HH the Amir said.

The discussions also covered regional developments in the Middle East, with both sides exchanging views on matters of mutual concern. Officials noted that the talks were held in a cordial atmosphere reflecting the strong ties between the two nations.

The visit of the Pakistani Prime Minister underscores the ongoing dialogue and partnership between Qatar and Pakistan, which have maintained diplomatic relations for decades. The two countries have collaborated on trade, investment, energy, and labour matters.

HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif's visit to Qatar is part of a broader effort to strengthen bilateral relations and explore new avenues for cooperation. The meeting at the Amiri Diwan reaffirms the commitment of both leaderships to further elevate the partnership.

Qatar and Pakistan continue to work together on regional stability and economic development, with both sides expressing optimism about the future of their bilateral ties.

package.instagram_caption: HH the Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani received HE Mr Shehbaz Sharif, Prime Minister of Pakistan, at the Amiri Diwan on Saturday. The leaders discussed strengthening bilateral ties and regional developments. #Qatar #Pakistan #Diplomacy #AmiriDiwan #BilateralRelations #QatarLiving

package.brief: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art opens 'Resolutions' exhibition to mark its 15th anniversary, exploring the museum's foundation through four key areas. The exhibition highlights the origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, exhibition history, knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and educational programmes. Entry is free for all visitors.

article.headline: Mathaf Opens 'Resolutions' Exhibition Marking 15 Years of the Museum
article.body:
Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art has opened a new exhibition titled 'Resolutions' to commemorate its 15th anniversary, according to officials from Qatar Museums.

The exhibition examines Mathaf's foundation through four defining areas: its origins under Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, its exhibition history, its engagement with knowledge circulation in the Arab world, and its educational programmes.

'Resolutions' offers visitors an in-depth look at the museum's journey since its inception, highlighting the vision that has shaped Mathaf into a leading institution for modern and contemporary Arab art.

The first area focuses on the origins of the collection, which began with Sheikh Hassan bin Mohammed bin Ali Al Thani, whose passion for art laid the groundwork for the museum.

The second area traces the exhibition history of Mathaf, showcasing key shows that have defined its programming over the years.

The third area explores the museum's role in fostering knowledge circulation across the Arab world, connecting artists, scholars, and audiences.

The fourth area highlights Mathaf's educational programmes, which have engaged diverse communities and nurtured new generations of art enthusiasts.

Entry to the 'Resolutions' exhibition is free for all visitors, in line with Mathaf's commitment to accessibility and cultural enrichment.

The exhibition underscores Qatar's dedication to preserving and promoting Arab modern art, further cementing the nation's status as a cultural hub in the region.

'Resolutions' is now open to the public at Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art, offering a reflective journey through 15 years of artistic achievement and institutional growth.

package.instagram_caption: Mathaf: Arab Museum of Modern Art opens 'Resolutions' exhibition, marking 15 years of the museum. Explore the origins, history, and impact of this cultural landmark through four defining areas. Entry is free for all visitors. #Mathaf #QatarMuseums #ArabModernArt #DohaCulture #Exhibition #FreeEntry

package.brief: The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has launched a summer mosquito-control programme deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts. Coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality, the initiative runs through October and aims to reduce mosquito-borne diseases. The programme underscores Qatar's proactive public health measures and shared community responsibility.

article.headline: Ministry of Public Health launches summer mosquito-control programme across 18 districts
article.body:
The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has launched a comprehensive summer mosquito-control programme, deploying drones and biological fish-based larvicide across 18 districts, officials announced.

The initiative, which runs through October, is coordinated with the Ministry of Municipality and aims to reduce mosquito populations and prevent the spread of vector-borne diseases during the warmer months.

According to the MoPH, the programme utilises advanced drone technology to survey and treat hard-to-reach breeding sites, while biological larvicide containing natural fish species is introduced into standing water bodies to target mosquito larvae.

“Public-health vigilance during summer is a shared responsibility,” said the MoPH Director of Communicable Diseases in a statement. The official emphasised that community cooperation is essential for the success of the programme.

The 18 districts were selected based on historical data on mosquito activity and population density. The MoPH urged residents to eliminate stagnant water around homes and report potential breeding sites.

This year’s programme builds on previous seasonal campaigns and reflects Qatar’s commitment to proactive public health measures. The Ministry of Municipality is supporting the effort by coordinating waste management and drainage maintenance.

Health officials noted that the programme aligns with national strategies to enhance environmental health and reduce disease transmission. Regular monitoring and evaluation will be conducted throughout the summer.

The MoPH will continue to provide updates and guidance to the public as the programme progresses.

package.instagram_caption: The Ministry of Public Health has launched its summer mosquito-control programme, using drones and biological larvicide across 18 districts. The initiative, in coordination with the Ministry of Municipality, runs through October. Let's do our part—eliminate standing water and report breeding sites. #Qatar #PublicHealth #MosquitoControl #SummerSafety #MoPH #QatarLiving
package.story_copy: MoPH launches summer mosquito-control programme using drones and biological larvicide across 18 districts. Runs through October.
package.push_notification: MoPH launches summer mosquito-control programme across 18 districts using drones and biological larvicide.

package.brief: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May and is being marked as completed on the events page. The expo brought together education leaders to discuss innovation and leadership in Qatar's education sector. This update ensures accurate event lifecycle management on Qatar Living.

article.headline: Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 Concludes, Marked as Completed
article.body:
The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 concluded on 24 May, bringing together key stakeholders from the education sector to discuss leadership and innovation, according to event organisers.

The expo, which has been listed on the Qatar Living events page, is now being moved from EventScheduled to Completed status as part of standard editorial operations, officials said.

During the event, participants explored strategies to enhance educational outcomes and foster leadership development in line with Qatar National Vision 2030. The gathering featured discussions on curriculum innovation, technology integration, and capacity building for educators.

Industry experts and ministry representatives shared insights on shaping the future of education in Qatar, emphasising the importance of collaborative efforts between public and private institutions.

The transition to Completed status ensures that the Qatar Living events calendar remains accurate and up to date for users seeking information on past and upcoming events.

As Qatar continues to invest in education as a cornerstone of national development, events like the Qatar Education Leadership Expo play a vital role in driving progress and excellence in the sector.

package.instagram_caption: The Qatar Education Leadership Expo 2026 has concluded, marking another milestone in advancing education leadership in Qatar. package.brief: Qatar Airways announced it will resume direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, from 15 July 2026, expanding its global network to over 160 destinations. The move strengthens connectivity between Doha and key markets in Europe and Asia, offering more travel options for passengers. The airline's Chief Commercial Officer highlighted the strategic importance of these routes.

article.headline: Qatar Airways to Resume Flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July 15
article.body:
Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of direct flights to Helsinki, Finland, and Tokyo Haneda, Japan, starting 15 July 2026, according to a statement from the airline.

The Helsinki route will initially operate four times weekly, increasing to daily services from 1 August 2026. Tokyo Haneda will receive four weekly flights from 15 July, complementing the existing daily service to Tokyo Narita.

"We are pleased to restore connectivity with Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda — two strategically important markets for our network," said Qatar Airways Chief Commercial Officer in a statement.

The expansion brings Qatar Airways' global network to over 160 destinations this summer, reinforcing its position as one of the world's leading carriers.

Helsinki serves as a key gateway to Scandinavia and the Baltic region, while Tokyo Haneda offers convenient access to central Tokyo, enhancing travel options for business and leisure passengers.

The resumption reflects Qatar Airways' commitment to rebuilding its network and meeting growing demand for international travel, officials said.

Passengers can book flights via the airline's website or travel agents, with competitive fares available for the new routes.

The announcement comes as Qatar continues to strengthen its aviation sector, a cornerstone of the national economy under the Qatar National Vision 2030.

package.instagram_caption: Qatar Airways is expanding its network! From July 15, fly direct to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda. Helsinki starts with four weekly flights, becoming daily from August. Tokyo Haneda gets four weekly flights, complementing Narita service. Book now! #QatarAirways #Helsinki #TokyoHaneda #Travel #Aviation #Qatar
package.story_copy: Qatar Airways resumes flights to Helsinki and Tokyo Haneda from July 15. Four weekly flights to each, with Helsinki going daily from August. Network now over 160 destinations.
